sections.paragraph:
fut. יַעֲבֹט-

(1) to change, to exchange (see Pi.); kindred is עָבַת to interweave.

(2) to give a pledge for anything borrowed (which includes the idea of exchange). Deu 24:10, לַעֲבֹט עֲבֹטֹו “that he may give his pledge.” Also to borrow on the security of a pledge, Deu 15:6, וְאַתָּה לֹא תַעֲבֹט “and thou shalt not borrow.”

Piel, to change, to exchange. Joe 2:7, “they shall not change their ways,” i.e. they shall go right on in the same way.

Hiphil, to lend [on security of a pledge], followed by acc. of pers. (to whom), Deu 15:6 followed by two acc. of pers. and the thing lent, Deu 15:8.

Hence עֲבֹוט and-
